name: Kenny Wheeler
description: Canadian composer and trumpet and flugelhorn player (1930–2014)
abstract: Kenneth Vincent John Wheeler, OC (14 January 1930 – 18 September 2014) was a Canadian composer and trumpet and flugelhorn player, based in the U.K. from the 1950s onwards.

concerts.label: Weill Recital Hall Centennial Festival: Rhythm In Mind

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I might be playing on a record with Kenny Wheeler and then there was Jack DeJohnette... Kenny would play with somebody else
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions a record with 'Lovano and Kenny Wheeler and Mark Feldman', but the interviewee does not confirm personal involvement or relationship with Wheeler, only discussing the band's general approach.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: He’s one of the relatively few trumpet players that you’ve played in a two-horn situation with. You’ve done Miles of course and Kenny Wheeler.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: He’d call me up and say, “I just got these charts from Kenny Wheeler, do you want to be come over and read through these charts?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 7, 2023
